content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all three review amendments: (1) Wrapped both force_kill and wait in a single tokio::time::timeout so the non-Unix force_kill path (which internally waits) is also bounded by the 30s timeout. (2) Replaced the non-deterministic test with two tests: managed_child_wait_respects_timeout deterministically exercises the timeout path by keeping a cat process alive via piped stdin without killing it, proving tokio::time::timeout correctly bounds ManagedChild::wait(); background_reap_drops_handle is an end-to-end test through spawn_background_reap_with_timeout with clear documentation that it validates Arc drop on both the happy and timeout paths.

## Steps

0. [Completed] Read current state of spawn_background_reap_with_timeout and both test functions from round 1
1. [Completed] Restructure spawn_background_reap_with_timeout to wrap both force_kill() and wait() in a single tokio::time::timeout, fixing amendment 2 (non-Unix force_kill hangs)
2. [Completed] Replace original test with managed_child_wait_respects_timeout: spawns cat with piped stdin, never kills it, asserts timeout fires on wait() — deterministically exercises the timeout code path (fixes amendments 1 and 3)
3. [Completed] Add background_reap_drops_handle: end-to-end test through spawn_background_reap_with_timeout with clear doc comment explaining it validates Arc drop on both paths
4. [Completed] Fix cargo fmt issue (line break in timeout call)
5. [Completed] Verify both new tests pass individually
6. [Completed] Verify full test suite: 756 passed, 0 failed
7. [Completed] Verify no clippy regressions in process_backend.rs
8. [Completed] Verify cargo fmt --check passes

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test managed_child_wait_respects_timeout: 1 passed (deterministically hits timeout because cat is alive and unkilled)
- cargo test background_reap_drops_handle: 1 passed (Arc dropped after reap task completes)
- cargo test: 756 passed, 0 failed, 9 ignored
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings | grep process_backend: no output (no new clippy warnings)
- cargo fmt --check: clean

## Outstanding Risks

- On platforms where SIGKILL is truly ineffective (uninterruptible I/O 'D' state), the process remains as a zombie after handle drop — this is inherent to the OS and the timeout correctly avoids blocking the tokio runtime
- Pre-existing clippy warnings (16 in other files) remain unfixed — not introduced by this change
